name: Dry Oatmeal Mix
directions: In a large bowl, combine the first eight ingredients.
Store in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 6 months.
Yield 8 cups total.
To prepare oatmeal: Shake mix well and measure out oatmeal into bowl, add water, and microwave for 3 minutes.
Or on a stove top, in a saucepan, bring water to a boil; slowly stir in 1/2 cup mix.
Cook and stir over medium heat for 1 minute.
Remove from the heat.
Cover and let stand for 1 minute or until oatmeal reaches desired consistency.

name: Sweet Morning Oatmeal
directions: Combine oats hot water and maple syrup in 3 cup bowl.
Cook on low power (I used 20%) for 9 minutes.
Allow to rest for 1 minute.
Add remaining ingredients, stir well, enjoy!
You can also use other oatmeal, just follow the recipe on the box. I have used rough cut oats with great results.

name: Hot Cereal
directions: Boil water & apple, when boiling add oats.
Cook 2 minutes & take off heat & cover for 3 minutes.
Spoon into bowls & add brown sugar & milk.

similar.name: Healthy Breakfast Oat Bran and Fruit

name: Basic Oatmeal
directions: Place 3/4 cup of the rolled oats into a blender and process until a flour.
Add all rolled oats, water, cinnamon and vanilla to pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and cook for 3 minutes.
Add honey and applesauce and stir for 1 minute.
Add to bowls.
Enjoy.

similar.name: Healthy Breakfast Oat Bran and Fruit

name: Flax Oatmeal
directions: Mix first 5 ingredients in a microwave safe bowl.
Microwave on high for 2 minutes
Stir oatmeal, and add flaxseed (it will thicken the oatmeal so don't worry if the mixture seems thin at first!)
Top with optional Splenda packets. I also top mine with more sliced banana & low carb vanilla yogurt. Raisins or nuts would be good, too!

name: Hearty Oatmeal
directions: In a microwave safe bowl, combine the oats, water, flax and cinnamon. If you don't want to make a mess of more dishes, just swirl the ingredients around in the bowl for a couple of seconds instead of stirring to mix them up.
Heat on high for 4 minutes.
After removing, stir in the yogurt and fruit (if desired) and eat! An easy way to change up the recipe is to vary the fruit, yogurt (with other flavours or cottage cheese) and/or spices.

Caution: Old-fashioned rolled oats have a tendency to grow exponentially in the microwave while cooking; be sure to use a bowl at least 3-4 times the size of the ingredients. I use a medium glass saucepan or corning ware dish.

name: Apple Oatsie Bran
directions: Mix oats, milk, applesauce, and water (as needed).
Microwave for 1 minute.
Stir in bran, grated apple, cinnamon, and extra water.
Cook, stirring each minute, for 3-4 minutes.
Stir in vanilla.
Serve.

name: Healthy Everyday Breakfast
directions: Place all ingredients in a bowl and stir.
Enjoy!

similar.name: Healthy Breakfast Oat Bran and Fruit

name: Perfect Microwave Oatmeal for One
directions: Put everything into a microwave safe bowl. You need one deep enough to avoid boil-over messes.
Microwave on HIGH for 2 minutes.
Stir and eat with a little milk and a sliced half banana. Yum!

name: Eggy Oatmeal
directions: Microwave oatmeal and water for approximately 2 minutes or until combined.
Stir in egg substitute, cinnamon, vanilla, sugar, and salt.
Return to microwave for approximately 1 minute or until desired consistency.
Can also be done on stove-top.

name: Granola
directions: In large mixing bowl, combine water and brown sugar. Microwave on high for 2-3 minutes, until sugar is dissolved. Add salt and vanilla and stir. Add oats and almonds, stir to coat. Spread in thin layer on baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Cook at 275F for 40-45 minutes. Cool and store in airtight container.

similar.name: Healthy Breakfast Oat Bran and Fruit

name: Healthy Breakfast Oat Bran and Fruit
directions: Prepare oat bran as directed (can use rolled oats too).
In the bowl you are going to eat out of, put the Pumpkin, Yogurt and Cinnamon, then add cooked oat bran.
Stir until mixed.
Sprinkle the blueberries and raspberries on top.
Enjoy.
